label: Outlook Calendar
description: rcade.dev LLM tools for Outlook Calendar

tools.name: CreateEvent
tools.description:
Create an event in the authenticated user's default calendar.

Ignores timezone offsets provided in the start_date_time and end_date_time parameters.
Instead, uses the user's default calendar timezone to filter events.
If the user has not set a timezone for their calendar, then the timezone will be UTC.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The text of the event's subject (title) line.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the event

parameters.name: start_date_time
parameters.description: The datetime of the event's start, represented in ISO 8601 format. Timezone offset is ignored. For example, 2025-04-25T13:00:00

parameters.name: end_date_time
parameters.description: The datetime of the event's end, represented in ISO 8601 format. Timezone offset is ignored. For example, 2025-04-25T13:30:00

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The location of the event

parameters.name: attendee_emails
parameters.description: The email addresses of the attendees of the event. Must be valid email addresses e.g., username@domain.com.

parameters.name: is_online_meeting
parameters.description: Whether the event is an online meeting. Defaults to False

parameters.name: custom_meeting_url
parameters.description: The URL of the online meeting. If not provided and is_online_meeting is True, then a url will be generated for you

output.description: A dictionary containing the created event details

body.value: Discuss project updates and next steps.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetEvent
tools.description: Get an event by its ID from the user's calendar.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The ID of the event to get

output.description: A dictionary containing the event details

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListCalendars
tools.description:
List all calendars the user has access to.

Returns the user's own calendars plus any shared or delegated calendars.
Each calendar includes its ID, name, owner, and permissions.

Use a calendar_id from the results to target a specific calendar
in other calendar tools.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of calendars to return. Max is 200. Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request.

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of available calendars.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListEventAttachments
tools.description:
List attachment metadata for a calendar event.

Returns metadata only (name, size, type, etc.). Attachment content is not included.
Use this tool when the user wants to know what files are attached to a calendar event
or meeting.

Pass a calendar_id to list attachments on events in shared or delegated calendars.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The ID of the calendar event to list attachments for.

parameters.name: calendar_id
parameters.description: ID of a specific calendar containing the event, including shared and delegated ones. When omitted, searches the user's default calendar.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of attachments to return. Max is 100. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request.

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of attachment metadata objects.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListEventsInTimeRange
tools.description:
List events in the user's calendar in a specific time range.

Ignores timezone offsets provided in the start_date_time and end_date_time parameters.
Instead, uses the user's default calendar timezone to filter events.
If the user has not set a timezone for their calendar, then the timezone will be UTC.

parameters.name: start_date_time
parameters.description: The start date and time of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format. Timezone offset is ignored. For example, 2025-04-24T19:00:00

parameters.name: end_date_time
parameters.description: The end date and time of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format. Timezone offset is ignored. For example, 2025-04-24T19:30:00

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of events to return. Max 1000. Defaults to 10

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of events

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchEvents
tools.description:
Search calendar events within a time range with optional filters.

Returns events filtered by subject keyword, attendee, organizer,
importance, or online-meeting status. Results are in chronological order.

By default, searches the user's primary calendar. Pass a calendar_id
to search a shared or delegated calendar.

Event bodies are truncated to 200 characters for efficient skimming.
Use the event_id from the results to retrieve full event details.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Filter events whose subject contains this text (case-insensitive). Defaults to None (no subject filter).

parameters.name: attendee_emails
parameters.description: Filter events that include these attendees by exact email address. Multiple emails are combined with AND (all must be attendees). Defaults to None (no attendee filter).

parameters.name: organizer_name
parameters.description: Filter events organized by this person's display name. Note: filtering by organizer email address is not supported by the Microsoft Graph API. Use the organizer's display name instead. Defaults to None (no organizer filter).

parameters.name: importance
parameters.description: Filter by event importance level. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: is_online_meeting
parameters.description: Filter for online meetings (True) or in-person meetings (False). Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: start_date_time
parameters.description: The start of the time range, in ISO 8601 format. Timezone offset is ignored; the user's calendar timezone is used. Defaults to None (90 days before today).

parameters.name: end_date_time
parameters.description: The end of the time range, in ISO 8601 format. Timezone offset is ignored; the user's calendar timezone is used. Defaults to None (90 days after today).

parameters.name: calendar_id
parameters.description: ID of a specific calendar to search, including shared and delegated ones. When omitted, searches the user's default calendar.

parameters.name: include_cancelled
parameters.description: Whether to include cancelled events in the results. Defaults to False.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return. Max is 200. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request.

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of matching calendar events.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description: Get information about the current user and their Outlook Calendar environment.

output.description: Get comprehensive user profile and Outlook Calendar information.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

documentationChunks.content:
The Arcade Outlook Calendar MCP Server uses the [Microsoft auth provider](/references/auth-providers/microsoft) to connect to users' Microsoft accounts.
---

summary:
Arcade.dev Outlook Calendar toolkit gives LLMs programmatic access to a user's Microsoft Outlook Calendar, enabling creation, querying, and management of events and related metadata. It supports reading calendars, searching and filtering events across time ranges, inspecting attachment metadata, and retrieving account context.

**Capabilities**
- Create and update events and retrieve full event details while honoring the user's calendar timezone.
- Search and list events with time-range, keyword, attendee, organizer, importance, and online-meeting filters.
- Enumerate accessible calendars and list attachment metadata for events to inform downstream actions.
- Provide user and calendar environment info for context-aware operations.

**OAuth**
Provider: microsoft
Scopes: Calendars.Read, Calendars.ReadBasic, Calendars.ReadWrite, MailboxSettings.Read, User.Read
